normalised-stop-reason.rule: Provider finish_reason/stop_reason must be mapped to exactly one of: 'stop' | 'length' | 'tool_calls' | 'unknown'. Any unrecognised value maps to 'unknown'. The mapping must be defined in a lookup table or switch (not inline conditionals).

drizzle-upsert-increment.rule: Accumulator columns (inputTokens, outputTokens, toolCallCount, modelCalls) must use sql`${col} + ${n}` in onConflictDoUpdate SET. New columns that are NOT accumulators (stopReason, max_tokens) must not use the increment pattern.

tool-error-throw.rule: All operational errors thrown by the orchestrator must use: throw new ToolError(CODE, message) where CODE is a member of AgentMcpErrorCode. Direct Error throws are forbidden inside the orchestrator loop.

provider-error-dispatch.rule: Provider catch blocks must dispatch to the correct error code in priority order: (1) AbortError/TimeoutError or signal.aborted → PROVIDER_TIMEOUT; (2) HTTP 401 / AuthenticationError → PROVIDER_AUTH_ERROR; (3) HTTP 429 after retries → PROVIDER_RATE_LIMITED; (4) context_length_exceeded → CONTEXT_WINDOW_EXCEEDED; (5) anything else → PROVIDER_ERROR. The order is mandatory — timeout must be detected before auth.

claudecli-auth-recovery.rule: When keychain extraction fails in ClaudeCliProvider or AnthropicProvider (OAuth path), the thrown PROVIDER_AUTH_ERROR message must include the recovery instruction: 'Set ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN (run `claude setup-token` to obtain an OAuth access token) or use authTokenEnv in the provider config'. The keychain error message must be included in the thrown message, not swallowed.
